description: Killed assumption: VR is not the causal lever for GPTZero human-passing. Prompt regime is. Pivoted product framing from 'voice preservation metric' to 'validated assembly prompt with VR as downstream diagnostic.'
notes: Pre-registered 54-variant pilot (n=54) surfaced the finding. Clarity 0.9 because the null is definitive on the sub-claim. Investment-avoided revised 0 → 12h on 2026-04-14: had we shipped claiming 35% VR as the causal mechanism and been challenged in a Part 2 interview ('but your pilot shows same-VR variants split across bands'), the live defense would have required redoing the pilot under pressure — ~10h research scramble + ~2h eval-methodology re-write. Pivoting pre-empted that entire failure mode.

activity: v4 framework port + UI bundle + regenerate-with-feedback workflow + README submission rewrite + edit-chat fixes (Day 5 MVP push)
notes: Session covered: v4 framework content port (moment-hook + bullets + conditional gap + 5-section framing), v4.1 calibration after k=3 + GPTZero scoring revealed prompt variance dwarfs prompt deltas, Edit Chat cancel + voice + Esc fixes, README rewritten for submission framing (MoJo Score story up top, multi-mode roadmap honest scope), UI bundle Enter-to-send + voice autoscroll + drop cap removal + inline API key validation, regenerate-with-voice-feedback workflow + upload-to-edit mode (replaces paragraph-level Edit Chat for MVP). 14 commits. 127 tests still green. Production build clean. Agent runtime (~3h of orchestration + verification calls) EXCLUDED per Part 3 — only counts user-facing prompting + reviewing time.

description: Reversed earlier 'GPTZero is just a noisy threshold gate' framing. A product called Human Writer Pro must reliably pass GPTZero — verbatim-stitched human writing being flagged AI is a brand-level failure, not just measurement noise. Optimization target = Mixed % (per user). Within-draft VR iteration still holds; cross-prompt VR comparison is still meaningless.
notes: User pushback after I framed GPTZero as 'too noisy to act on' during the v4.1 vs v3 comparison. Reversal saved an estimated 5h of energy that would have gone toward iterating prompts on framework adherence alone without addressing the GPTZero pass-rate variance — the actual product-quality bar. Clarity 0.75 because the optimization target (Mixed %) is unvalidated and we don't have automated GPTZero regression yet to pressure-test. See feedback_gptzero_is_the_bar.md (career-forge memory).

description: Replaced paragraph-level Edit Chat (Socratic complaint→question→answer→restitch on selected paragraphs) with whole-output regenerate-with-voice-feedback as the MVP edit functionality. Matches the proven Career-Forge dashboard workflow (eye-test → voice ask for changes → artifact regenerates). Selection-based Edit Chat with single-word inline editing is the right post-MVP design, but the simpler regen-with-feedback ships and demos cleanly in the 8-hour window.
notes: Direct user spec 2026-04-15. Avoided ~10h of selection-refactor work (selection range tracking, anchor-based replacement instead of indexOf, two-paradigm UI for word-vs-paragraph selections, inline popover edits with contenteditable). Clarity 0.85 because the workflow is empirically validated (Career Forge dashboard usage) and the user's 4 minimum requirements were met without the harder feature. See project_edit_chat_selection_scope.md (career-forge memory) for the deferred refactor scope.

description: Shipped v4.1 framework content port despite GPTZero variance (1/3 pass rate across k=3 on the CrowdStrike fixture). Three rounds of k=3 (v3 / v4 rigid / v4.1 loose+capped) all showed 1-2/3 pass with mean human% 15-40% — variance dwarfs prompt deltas. v3 Letter 1 at 99% human and v4 Rep 2 at 83% human prove the structure works; the fixture's dense technical cybersec content register is the GPTZero flip driver, not the prompt. Eye-test on v4.1 confirmed framework adherence (moment-hook, bullets, conditional gap, named company 2x, banned phrases absent) is materially better than v3's wall-of-text prose.
notes: Avoided ~6h of additional prompt iteration that would not have moved the GPTZero needle (variance > effect size). Clarity 0.7 because the decision rests on user's eye-test prioritization over GPTZero noise — defensible but not pre-registered. The roughness-injection pass (separate experiment) is the right post-MVP path for actually narrowing the variance band. See project_voice_match_vs_ai_smoothness.md (career-forge memory).

description: Pivoted Mojo take-home submission framing: HWP is the project being submitted (not Career Forge). Career Forge = multi-agent orchestration pipeline (engineering depth); HWP = productized output of one component (productization instinct). Together the story is 'I built the orchestration, then productized the load-bearing piece for end-users.' Multi-mode polish (academic, email, free-form) deferred; mode hardcoded to cover-letter for MVP demo.
notes: User decision 2026-04-15 mid-MVP push. Avoided ~4h of mode-picker restoration + per-mode prompt tuning + per-mode UI testing. README documents multi-mode as roadmap honestly rather than over-promising in the demo. Clarity 0.8 because the scoping is clear and the tradeoff (focused single-mode demo vs feature-incomplete multi-mode) is well-understood; only post-submission feedback would tell us if the scoping was correct.

description: Deferred inline text editing of assembled output. Textarea-swap retrofit would be a downgrade from the selection-based single-word inline editing design (documented in project_edit_chat_selection_scope.md). Regenerate-with-feedback already covers the 'change one thing' use case — heavier but functional. The abuse vector for VR denominator (user pastes ChatGPT text → counted as user-authored) is resolvable via numerator-only recalc, but not worth the pre-submission UAT surface area.
notes: User-driven scope decision 2026-04-16. Avoided ~3h of textarea retrofit + state sync + VR recalc wiring + UAT cycles that would have been replaced by the selection-based refactor anyway. Clarity 0.85 because both the alternate design (selection-based) and the current substitute (regen-with-feedback) are empirically validated; deferral is unambiguously the right move.

description: Scoped 'Ignore AI-isms' feature down from surgical preserve-verbatim override to dismiss-only button. The detector is pattern-matching; some users' natural voices legitimately hit patterns (false-positives are unavoidable at this scope). Rather than spend ~1.5h on prompt engineering + testing a preserve-verbatim system prompt override, ship the 3-line UI change that clears the current batch.
notes: User-stated: 'we'll spend too much time on this to do it surgically... I know myself enough that that's an important decision value.' Clarity 0.9 because the user explicitly recognized the scope-creep trap and named the Decision Value tradeoff in real-time. Scoped-down version shipped in commit 4c943b1 (2 files, 37 insertions).
